en: Think learning English is always perfect and aesthetic?
en_example: Think learning English is always perfect and aesthetic?
zh_example: Think learning English is always perfect and aesthetic?
ja_example: Think learning English is always perfect and aesthetic?

en: Every single morning, I rise with the sun.
en_example: Every single morning, I rise with the sun.
zh_example: Every single morning, I rise with the sun.
ja: 日の出とともに起きる
ja_example: Every single morning, I rise with the sun.

zh: 貪睡按鈕（鬧鐘的延遲響鈴按鈕）
en: But actually, I hit the snooze button 10 times.
en_example: But actually, I hit the snooze button 10 times.
zh_example: But actually, I hit the snooze button 10 times.
ja: スヌーズボタン（目覚まし時計の一時停止ボタン）
ja_example: But actually, I hit the snooze button 10 times.

en: For breakfast, I choose a nutritious smoothie bowl.
en_example: For breakfast, I choose a nutritious smoothie bowl.
zh_example: For breakfast, I choose a nutritious smoothie bowl.
ja_example: For breakfast, I choose a nutritious smoothie bowl.

zh: 大腦的燃料（比喻食物提供能量）
en: It is fuel for my brain.
en_example: It is fuel for my brain.
zh_example: It is fuel for my brain.
ja: 脳の燃料（比喩的に食べ物がエネルギーを与えること）
ja_example: It is fuel for my brain.

zh: 現實檢驗；現實狀況的提醒
en: Reality check I'm starving, so I'm eating this cold pizza for Monday.
en_example: Reality check I'm starving, so I'm eating this cold pizza for Monday.
zh_example: Reality check I'm starving, so I'm eating this cold pizza for Monday.
ja: 現実の確認；現実を直視すること
ja_example: Reality check I'm starving, so I'm eating this cold pizza for Monday.

en: Now it's time to master English with zero distractions.
en_example: Now it's time to master English with zero distractions.
zh_example: Now it's time to master English with zero distractions.
ja: 気を散らすもの；注意をそらすこと
ja_example: Now it's time to master English with zero distractions.

en: Focus and discipline are the keys to success.
en_example: Focus and discipline are the keys to success.
zh_example: Focus and discipline are the keys to success.
ja_example: Focus and discipline are the keys to success.

en: I am a master of procrastination.
en_example: I am a master of procrastination.
zh_example: I am a master of procrastination.
ja: 先延ばし；ぐずぐずすること
ja_example: I am a master of procrastination.

en: No pain, no gain.
en_example: No pain, no gain.
zh_example: No pain, no gain.
ja: 苦労なくして得るものなし
ja_example: No pain, no gain.

zh: 保持身材；保持健康狀態
en: Staying in shape is my top priority.
en_example: Staying in shape is my top priority.
zh_example: Staying in shape is my top priority.
ja_example: Staying in shape is my top priority.

en: Let's push the limits.
en_example: Let's push the limits.
zh_example: Let's push the limits.
ja_example: Let's push the limits.

en: Does walking to the fridge count as cardio?
en_example: Does walking to the fridge count as cardio?
zh_example: Does walking to the fridge count as cardio?
ja_example: Does walking to the fridge count as cardio?

en: Having deep conversations in fluent English.
en_example: Having deep conversations in fluent English.
zh_example: Having deep conversations in fluent English.
ja_example: Having deep conversations in fluent English.

zh: 筋疲力盡的；非常疲倦的
en: The truth? I'm exhausted.
en_example: The truth? I'm exhausted.
zh_example: The truth? I'm exhausted.
ja_example: The truth? I'm exhausted.

en: This is my perfect me time.
en_example: This is my perfect me time.
zh_example: This is my perfect me time.
ja_example: This is my perfect me time.

en: Wake up is opening your eyes, but get up is leaving the bed.
en_example: Wake up is opening your eyes, but get up is leaving the bed.
zh_example: Wake up is opening your eyes, but get up is leaving the bed.
ja: 目を覚ますことと起き上がることの違い
ja_example: Wake up is opening your eyes, but get up is leaving the bed.

zh: 餓到生氣的狀態（飢餓+生氣）
en: When you are so hungry that you get angry, you are hangry.
en_example: When you are so hungry that you get angry, you are hangry.
zh_example: When you are so hungry that you get angry, you are hangry.
ja: 空腹で怒りっぽくなる状態（hungry + angry）
ja_example: When you are so hungry that you get angry, you are hangry.

en: To procrastinate means to delay your work.
en_example: To procrastinate means to delay your work.
zh_example: To procrastinate means to delay your work.
ja_example: To procrastinate means to delay your work.

en: Are you a procrastinator?
en_example: Are you a procrastinator?
zh_example: Are you a procrastinator?
ja_example: Are you a procrastinator?

zh: 整天坐在沙發上不動的人
en: A couch potato is someone who stays on the sofa all day.
en_example: A couch potato is someone who stays on the sofa all day.
zh_example: A couch potato is someone who stays on the sofa all day.
ja: 一日中ソファに座っている怠け者
ja_example: A couch potato is someone who stays on the sofa all day.

en: Are you a gym rat or a couch potato?
en_example: Are you a gym rat or a couch potato?
zh_example: Are you a gym rat or a couch potato?
ja_example: Are you a gym rat or a couch potato?
